resources.string: Tap to select · Hold to favourite
Tap twice to exit · Hold to switch style

Philips Hue is a trademark of Signify. This app is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Signify.

LIFX is a trademark of Lifi Labs, Inc.

Ableton Link is a trademark of Ableton AG.

Privacy Policy for Velo Visualiser

 Last Updated: June 19, 2026

 Velo Visualiser ("we", "our", or "us") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we handle data when you use our mobile application.

 1. Information We Collect

 Audio Data (Microphone and System Audio)
 • What we collect: The app accesses your device's microphone and system audio (via MediaProjection) to generate real-time visualisations.
 • How we use it: Audio data is processed locally and in real-time on your device. We use this data only to calculate frequency bands and waveforms for visual effects and haptic feedback.
 • Storage and Transmission: We do not record, store, or transmit any audio data. The raw PCM data exists only in temporary volatile memory (RAM) for the duration of the processing (typically less than 100ms) and is immediately overwritten.

 Network Information
 • Local Network Access: The app requests access to your local network to discover and communicate with Philips Hue Bridges and LIFX smart bulbs.
 • Usage: This access is used solely for local UDP/REST communication with your smart lighting hardware. No data is sent to external servers or the cloud.

 Device Information
 • The app may check your device's display capabilities (such as HDR support and refresh rate) to optimize the OpenGL rendering. This information is not collected or transmitted.

 2. Third-Party Services
 • Philips Hue: If you enable Hue Sync, the app communicates directly with your Philips Hue Bridge over your local Wi-Fi. Please refer to the Philips Hue privacy policy for information on how they handle data.
 • LIFX: If you use LIFX Sync, the app broadcasts UDP packets over your local Wi-Fi to discover and control your local LIFX bulbs. We do not transmit this network data to any cloud service. Please refer to the LIFX privacy policy for information on how their smart bulbs handle network traffic.

 3. Data Safety
 Velo Visualiser does not collect, share, or sell any personal data. It is a utility tool designed to operate entirely on-device.

 4. Changes to This Policy
 We may update our Privacy Policy from time to time.

 5. Contact Us
 If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us via our GitHub repository.
How the lights respond to the music
How readily a beat triggers — affects the visuals, lights and haptics together.
Locks the beat to music apps on your network (Ableton Live, Traktor…) instead of the mic — for dead-on, on-grid visuals.

Locks the beat to Ableton Link software on your Wi-Fi (Traktor, Ableton Live, and more) for perfectly-timed bloom and haptics. The microphone still drives the visuals — Link only sets the beat. Both devices must be on the same network.
With Link on, the visuals swell into each beat and snap on the hit — an anticipatory punch only a shared clock can time.
Link adds an anticipatory punch and bloom to the Reactive & Immersive visuals. The Instrument visuals stay a pure readout of the sound.
Link knows the beat grid but not where the bar\'s “1” is. If the lit dot above lands on the wrong beat, tap to nudge it onto your downbeat (beats stay aligned).

⚠ Experimental. Adds bar-synced glow and drop surges on top of the beat. These depend on your Ableton Link bar alignment and the track\'s dynamics, so they can misfire on some setups — leave off to keep the visuals on the beat only.

The line on each bar is the trigger point: beats fire when the level bar pushes past its line, and bass-heavy beats turn blue/purple when the bass bar is past its line (red below). How readily beats fire is shared with the visuals — set it under Settings ▸ Beat Sensitivity.

Tune the audio-reactive light show. The lights follow the music\'s spectrum; adjust how bright they are and how hard beats flash. How readily a beat registers is shared with the visuals — set it under Settings ▸ Beat Sensitivity.
Sends the beat command early to compensate for Hue (Zigbee) and LIFX (Wi-Fi) network latency, so the lights flash on time. Adjust until beats feel in sync.
Forces the display to maximum brightness and dynamic range for the best HDR punch. May increase heat and battery drain.
Forces the display to 100%% brightness for maximum visibility. May increase battery drain.
Requires a Philips Hue Bridge (v2), Color Lights, and an Entertainment Area configured in the official Hue app. Your phone and bridge must be on the same Wi-Fi.
WLED runs on DIY ESP32/ESP8266 LED controllers on your Wi-Fi. Make sure your phone is on the same network, then scan to find and sync your devices.
Works over your local Wi-Fi only — your phone and Hue Bridge must be on the same network. No Hue cloud account is used. Light sync uses the Microphone source.
Light sync works with the Microphone source. Switch the audio source to Microphone.
Press the button on your Hue Bridge… %1$d s
No bridge found. Make sure your phone and Hue Bridge are on the same Wi-Fi, then try again.
No Entertainment Areas found. Please open the official Philips Hue app and create one first.
Bridge not reachable. Check you\'re on the same Wi-Fi as your Hue Bridge.
You\'ll need to press the bridge button again to re-pair.

Nanoleaf panels/shapes support an ultra-fast Wi-Fi streaming protocol. (Note: Nanoleaf Essentials bulbs are Bluetooth/Matter and are not supported).\n\nTo pair, hold the power button on your Nanoleaf controller for 5-7 seconds until the LED starts flashing. Ensure your phone is on the same Wi-Fi network.

To visualize sound from other apps (music, video, games), Android routes their audio through its screen-capture API — so the system will ask for screen-recording permission.\n\nThis app only reads the audio that\'s playing. It never records, saves, or transmits your screen.
Internal audio has higher latency due to system buffering. For the best low-latency experience, use the Microphone.
◀ Swipe left / right to change visuals ▶
